title: What Is an AI Token? A Beginner's Complete Guide
excerpt: Every time you use ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini, you're billed in tokens — not words. This guide explains what tokens are, why they matter for cost, and how to think about them.

body.text: If you've ever used ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini and wondered why you're charged per 'token' rather than per word — you're not alone. Tokens are the fundamental unit of text that AI language models process.
The rule of thumb: 100 tokens ≈ 75 words in English.
Every AI API charges based on token count — both what you send and what the model generates back.

items: Input tokens — the text you send
Output tokens — the model's response (priced higher)
Context window — the maximum tokens a model can see at once
Choose models by task, not by brand

title: How to Calculate AI Token Costs Before You Commit
excerpt: Before running any AI workload at scale, you need to estimate token costs. Here's the exact method to forecast your monthly bill across any model.

body.text: Unexpected AI bills are one of the most common pain points for developers and businesses adopting AI APIs. A small miscalculation in token usage can turn a $50 prototype into a $5,000 monthly bill.
Cost = (input tokens × input price per 1M) + (output tokens × output price per 1M)
